data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 76,000 USDT with a Narrowed 0.03% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 29, 2026, 15:39 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 76,000 USDT and is now trading at 75,981.007813 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.03% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: WTI crude oil surged 6% intraday, hitting a new high since April 8.
data.description: According to Gate data, WTI crude oil surged 6.00% intraday, hitting a new high since April 8, currently trading at $107.49 per barrel. This followed data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) showing that the U.S. withdrew crude oil from its Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) in the latest week to its highest level since October 2022.

data.title: Analysts: Traders may reduce risk exposure ahead of the Fed decision.
data.description: Pepperstone analyst Michael Brown said traders are likely to reduce their exposure to risk assets ahead of the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision at 2 a.m. Beijing time on Thursday and earnings reports from US tech giants such as Microsoft, Alphabet (Google's parent company), Amazon, and Meta. He noted, "Given the looming risks of these events, many participants will want to cut their positions." He added that traders are also "realizing" there is still no good news regarding the Middle East conflict, with no sign of a US-Iran peace agreement and the Strait of Hormuz remaining blocked. (Jinshi)

data.title: Traders Anticipate Risk Reduction Ahead of Key Events
data.description: Pepperstone analyst Michael Brown has indicated that traders may reduce exposure to risk assets ahead of significant events. According to Odaily, the U.S. Federal Reserve is set to announce its interest rate decision at 2 a.m. UTC+8 on Thursday, alongside earnings reports from major U.S. tech companies such as Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, and Meta. Brown noted that many participants are likely to cut positions due to these impending risks. Additionally, traders are aware that there is no positive news regarding the Middle East conflict, with no signs of a peace agreement between the U.S. and Iran, and the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked.

data.title: Pantera Capital CEO Highlights Bitcoin's Undervaluation Amid AI Stock Surge
data.description: Pantera Capital CEO Dan Morehead has highlighted a significant undervaluation in Bitcoin, noting that it is currently trading 43% below its trend. According to NS3.AI, Morehead also pointed out that an internal index reveals leading AI companies are trading 33% above their trend. Despite broader adoption and regulatory advancements in the U.S., Morehead argued that institutional exposure to cryptocurrency remains limited, suggesting that the crypto market is still underpriced.

data.title: Robinhood's Crypto Revenue Declines in Q1 Amid Falling Trading Volume
data.description: Robinhood experienced a significant drop in its cryptocurrency transaction revenue during the first quarter, with a 47% decrease to $134 million. According to NS3.AI, the platform's crypto trading volume also fell by 48% to $24 billion, marking the third consecutive quarterly decline in crypto transaction revenue. Despite these challenges, Robinhood reported earnings per share of $0.38 and total revenue of $1.07 billion. Additionally, the company's net income saw a year-over-year increase of 3%, reaching $346 million.

data.title: Market pricing suggests that expectations for a Federal Reserve rate cut before 2027 have cooled significantly.
data.description: Kalshi's market pricing forecasts indicate that the market now sees only about a 50% probability of a Federal Reserve rate cut before 2027, a significant drop from the 80-90% probability earlier this year. As the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meets, the market is effectively pricing in a "higher interest rate environment for a longer period," suggesting a lack of confidence in near-term monetary easing.

data.title: Pentagon Reports $25 Billion Spent on Iran Conflict
data.description: The Pentagon has reported that the United States has spent approximately $25 billion on the conflict in Iran. According to Odaily, this information was disclosed during a hearing of the U.S. House Armed Services Committee. Acting Inspector General of the Department of Defense, Hurst, provided the most comprehensive cost estimate to date, informing lawmakers that the majority of the funds were allocated for ammunition. U.S. Secretary of Defense, Hegseth, also attended the hearing to provide testimony.

data.title: The Pentagon says the war with Iran has so far "burned" $25 billion.
data.description: The Pentagon stated that the United States has spent approximately $25 billion on the conflict with Iran. At a hearing before the House Armed Services Committee, Acting Comptroller General Hearst released this most comprehensive cost estimate to date, informing lawmakers that the majority of the money was spent on ammunition. Defense Secretary Hergsays also testified at the hearing. (Jinshi)

data.title: Microsoft's Earnings Report Expected Amid Stock Surge
data.description: Microsoft is set to announce its earnings on April 29, following a notable stock increase of approximately 20% from its March lows, reaching around $427. According to NS3.AI, analysts Rich Ross and Dan Ives have shared optimistic perspectives regarding the company's performance. However, John DiFucci has cautioned that Wall Street might have overly high expectations for Azure's growth in the third fiscal quarter.

data.title: Expert Highlights Importance of Guidance in Token Performance
data.description: Jordan Brewer emphasized that token teams providing specific guidance and successfully delivering on it can enhance token performance. According to NS3.AI, Brewer pointed to Maple's growth from $460 million in assets under management (AUM) to $5 billion, and SYRUP's increase from $0.10 to $0.60 as examples. Brewer also noted that institutions are increasingly separating custody from execution, with BlackRock's approximately $2.5 billion BUIDL fund being accepted as off-exchange collateral.

data.title: Bitcoin ETF Inflows Strengthen Institutional Positioning
data.description:
21Shares Chief Investment Officer Adrian Fritz has highlighted the ongoing inflow of funds into spot Bitcoin ETFs, reinforcing Bitcoin's role in institutional asset allocation, even as its price remains below $80,000. According to Odaily, Fritz noted that Bitcoin ETFs have attracted nearly $2 billion this year, with contributions from retail investors, institutions, and hedge funds engaging in arbitrage and options strategies. As traditional asset management firms like Morgan Stanley expand their involvement, cryptocurrencies are increasingly being integrated into multi-asset portfolios. Bitcoin's current daily trading volume exceeds $50 billion, with liquidity levels approaching those of major tech stocks like Nvidia. The ETF mechanism provides liquidity in both primary and secondary markets, enhancing its status as an 'institutional-grade asset.'

Despite macroeconomic and interest rate pressures, Fritz believes that ETF inflows have shifted from speculative to structural demand. He anticipates that improvements in geopolitical conditions, continued fund inflows, and short-covering could drive Bitcoin to challenge the $100,000 mark within the year. Meanwhile, the divergence among altcoins is intensifying, with the market shifting towards assets with strong fundamentals and cash flow considerations.

data.title: US senators push the Clarity bill into the review process, potentially clarifying stablecoin yield terms.
data.description: Crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett wrote on the X platform that U.S. Senator Thom Tillis plans to push the Clarity Bill to the Senate Banking Committee's markup stage as soon as possible, stating that legislative progress has "achieved substantial consensus" and should now move into the formal implementation process. Tillis told Congress that he will request the committee chairman to schedule a review meeting after the congressional recess and expects to release the legislative text regarding stablecoin yield provisions 4-5 days before the review, for industry and stakeholders to review in advance. Most banks' concerns about the risks associated with stablecoin yields have been addressed in the discussions, and he encouraged institutions with remaining opinions to "participate in good faith to improve the legislation." Furthermore, Tillis mentioned that he generally supports the legislative framework proposed by Senator Cynthia Lummis regarding issues such as the potential impact on software developers and law enforcement under the 1960 Criminal Law Act. This statement indicates that U.S. crypto regulatory legislation is accelerating into a substantial phase regarding the definition of stablecoin and developer responsibilities.

data.title: France Announces Plan to Phase Out Fossil Fuels by 2050
data.description: France has announced an ambitious plan to eliminate the use of fossil fuels by 2050, as revealed at a climate summit in Colombia. Bloomberg posted on X, detailing the country's strategy to transition away from coal by 2030, oil by 2045, and natural gas by 2050. This initiative is part of France's broader efforts to combat climate change and reduce carbon emissions. The plan underscores France's commitment to sustainable energy and aligns with global efforts to address environmental challenges.

data.title: 21Shares executive: Bitcoin may hit $100,000 this year; institutions are accelerating their entry.
data.description: Adrian Fritz, Chief Investment Officer of 21Shares, stated that the continued inflow of funds into spot Bitcoin ETFs is strengthening Bitcoin's core position in institutional asset allocation, even as prices continue to fluctuate below $80,000. Fritz pointed out that Bitcoin ETFs have absorbed nearly $2 billion in funds this year, with sources including retail investors, institutions, and hedge funds engaging in arbitrage and options strategies. He believes that as traditional asset management institutions like Morgan Stanley accelerate their deployments, crypto assets are being more widely incorporated into multi-asset portfolios. Bitcoin's current daily trading volume exceeds $50 billion, and its liquidity level is approaching that of large-cap tech stocks like Nvidia. The ETF mechanism provides liquidity in both the primary and secondary markets, gradually giving it the attributes of an "institutional-grade asset." Although the market remains constrained by the macroeconomic and interest rate environment, Fritz believes that ETF inflows have shifted from speculative-driven to structural demand, and he expects Bitcoin to challenge the $100,000 mark this year, driven by factors such as improved geopolitical conditions, continued capital inflows, and short covering. At the same time, altcoin differentiation is intensifying, and the market is shifting towards an asset selection logic that places greater emphasis on fundamentals and cash flow. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Analyzing Head and Shoulders and Double Bottom Patterns for Trading Strategies
data.description: The article explores the practical application of head and shoulders and double bottom patterns in trading. According to PANews, these patterns are analyzed in conjunction with Fibonacci extensions and balance line theory to develop high-probability trading strategies. The focus is on understanding these technical analysis tools to enhance decision-making in financial markets.

data.title: U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve Releases Hit Highest Level Since October 2022
data.description: The U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) releases have reached their highest level since October 2022, according to data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration. This increase in releases marks a significant development in the management of the nation's emergency oil stockpile.

data.title: Bank of Canada Governor Discusses Interest Rate Concerns Amid Oil Price Fluctuations
data.description: Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem expressed concerns about the potential consequences of raising interest rates in the current economic climate. According to Jin10, Macklem highlighted the risk of increasing rates now, only to face regret if oil prices decline later. He emphasized that the impact of rate hikes on the economy might become evident only after oil prices have already fallen, suggesting a cautious approach to monetary policy decisions.

data.title: Pakistan Expects Iran to Submit Revised Peace Proposal by Friday as Khamenei Access Delays Talks
data.description: Key TakeawaysPakistani mediators expect Iran to submit a revised peace proposal as early as Tuesday or by Friday at the latest, per CNN cited by BlockBeatsThe delay is attributed to difficulties in reaching Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, whose sign-off appears necessary for any formal responseTrump previously rejected an earlier version of Iran's peace offer, requiring Tehran to revise its proposalPakistani Prime Minister Sharif publicly stated that Iran's Foreign Minister personally assured him of a reply, adding diplomatic pressure on TehranThe White House has signaled its patience is wearing thin, raising the stakes for Iran's response windowPakistani mediators expect Iran to submit a revised peace proposal to the United States either later Tuesday or by Friday at the latest, CNN reported on April 29, as diplomatic efforts to end the US-Iran conflict enter a critical window with multiple parties signaling diminishing patience.The revised proposal is required after President Trump rejected an earlier version of Iran's peace offer. Sources familiar with the matter told CNN that Iran could respond as early as Tuesday, though delays have pushed the realistic window toward Friday -- with the uncertainty itself reflecting the difficulties negotiators are encountering in securing a formal response from Tehran.Khamenei Access Is the Core BottleneckSources indicated that the primary source of delay is difficulty in contacting Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, whose approval appears to be a prerequisite for any official Iranian position on revised peace terms. The inability to secure direct access to the ultimate decision-maker within Iran's fractured power structure has been a recurring obstacle throughout the negotiation process, with hardline IRGC factions also complicating the civilian government's ability to speak with a unified voice.Pakistan Applies Public PressurePakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif added a layer of public diplomatic pressure by telling his cabinet that Iran's Foreign Minister -- who led negotiations in Islamabad over the weekend -- personally assured him that a reply would be forthcoming. No specific timetable was attached to that assurance, but Sharif's decision to make the commitment public is a notable escalation of diplomatic pressure on Tehran.White House Patience Running ThinThe compressed timeline is further tightened by signals from Washington. The White House has indicated its patience is wearing thin, framing the Friday window as a de facto deadline rather than a flexible target. The combination of a truncated ceasefire extension, Khamenei access difficulties, and a visibly impatient White House makes the next 48–72 hours one of the most consequential diplomatic windows of the conflict.Crypto and Market ImplicationsFor financial markets including crypto, the Iran proposal timeline is a binary near-term catalyst. A revised proposal that gains White House approval could ease the geopolitical risk premium embedded in oil prices -- with Brent currently above $114 per barrel -- and provide a meaningful tailwind for Bitcoin and risk assets. A failure to submit or a second rejection would likely extend the energy price spike and keep macro conditions hostile for risk-on positioning heading into the May monthly open.

data.title: AI-powered financial platform Rogo has raised $160 million in Series D funding, led by Kleiner Perkins.
data.description: Rogo, an AI-powered financial workflow platform, announced the completion of a $160 million Series D funding round. The round was led by Kleiner Perkins, with participation from Sequoia Capital, Thrive Capital, Khosla Ventures, and J.P. Morgan Growth Equity Partners. Rogo stated that its AI system has been deployed in several top global investment banks, asset management firms, and private equity firms to improve investment research, modeling, and client communication efficiency. This funding round will be used to deepen system integration, expand its on-site engineering and investment banking teams, and accelerate expansion in the European and Asian markets. The company emphasized that AI is driving the "democratization and efficiency restructuring" of high-end financial services capabilities.
